# API Discovery and Extract

> ⚠️ **Stop. This document is incomplete by itself.**
> Before implementing anything, read [`SKILL.md`](../SKILL.md).
> It contains global rules, required namespaces, architecture constraints, and documentation navigation.
> Do not continue without reading it.

> You are here if you need to:
> - find the exact LinqToDB API for the installed package version
> - decide whether a typed API exists before using a generic fallback
> - inspect overload families, return types, XML-doc remarks, or AI-Tags
> - answer provider-specific API questions without inventing methods

---

## Core Rule

Markdown guides explain concepts, constraints, and common mistakes. They are not a complete public
API listing.

The package XML documentation is the exact current-version public API surface for members that have
XML comments:

```text
lib/<TFM>/linq2db.xml
```

Missing from markdown does not mean missing from LinqToDB. Do not conclude that an API is
unavailable until XML-doc has been searched.

If this package also ships a generated markdown API extract, use it as a search aid. The canonical
API shape is still the XML-doc member entry.

Do not read `docs/api.md` sequentially. It is a generated search index. Search by task terms,
provider name, SQL keyword, member name, receiver type, or AI-Tags.

Do not use `LinqToDB.Internal.*` APIs in application code. They are implementation details even
when visible as public members in the assembly or XML documentation. Consumer-facing API discovery
must use supported non-`Internal` `LinqToDB.*` surfaces.

`docs/api.md` is allowed to contain curated API extract sections generated from XML-doc. Those
sections are optimized for discovery, not for copying every XML member verbatim.

When `linq2db.xml` changes, the generated API extract in this file must be regenerated in the same
change. Do not manually patch generated extract rows except as a temporary diagnostic step; fix the
XML-doc source or the extract generator and regenerate.

---

## Discovery Workflow

Use this workflow for any API-level question:

1. Read the relevant task guide in `docs/*.md`.
2. Start with the narrowest and most specific API surface: provider-specific guides, maps,
   namespaces, and typed helpers.
3. Search the generated API extract by its `Search anchors:` lines first. Anchors are the primary
   discovery vocabulary for agents: they include provider names, SQL keywords, common task words,
   helper-name fragments, receiver scope, and AI-Tags.
4. Use headings, summaries, and AI-Tags to confirm likely candidates.
5. Only after a candidate is found, copy the `XML member` id from its table row.
6. Search `lib/<TFM>/linq2db.xml` by that exact XML member id.
7. Verify the exact member signature, receiver type, parameters, return type, remarks, and tags.
8. Prefer typed and provider-specific APIs found in XML-doc.
9. Use generic string-based APIs only when no typed API exists or when the typed API does not cover
   the requested case.
10. Use custom SQL, raw SQL, and interceptors only after typed and generic APIs do not cover the
   requested case.
11. If markdown and XML-doc disagree, prefer XML-doc for exact API shape and mention the discrepancy.

Do not invent APIs, overloads, options, provider flags, provider capabilities, XML-doc remarks, or
AI-Tags.

For SQL hint questions, this workflow has an additional gate: search `docs/hints-api-map.md`
and the provider `*Hints` XML-doc members before recommending `QueryHint`, `TableHint`,
`Sql.Expression`, raw SQL, or interceptors.

For negative hint lookups, be stricter than normal retrieval. Do not conclude that a typed helper is
absent from `docs/hints-api-map.md` unless you searched that map by exact provider and exact SQL or
database term. Then search `Search anchors:` in this file for the provider `*Hints` type, SQL term,
candidate helper fragments, `Group=Hints`, and `HintType=...` before using generic fallback APIs.

Hint lookup order is strict: map first, XML-doc verification second, typed provider helper third,
generic hint APIs fourth, custom SQL/interceptors last.
This applies even when the database feature is a table modifier, lock clause, query directive, or
provider-specific SQL extension rather than a classic optimizer hint.

---

## API Extract Contract

The agent-friendly API extract is intentionally more compact than `linq2db.xml`.

It should help agents find the right API family quickly, then verify exact details in XML-doc.
Do not treat a compact extract entry as the only overload that exists.
Do not use raw XML member ids as the first search vocabulary. They are exact lookup keys after a
candidate is found, not human- or agent-friendly retrieval text.

The generated extract is derived data. Its source of truth is `lib/<TFM>/linq2db.xml`, which in
turn is produced from public XML comments. Any change that affects XML-doc members, summaries,
remarks, parameter documentation, or AI-Tags must also refresh this extract before the package is
shipped.

The generated extract must include only consumer-supported LinqToDB API members whose XML-doc
member id starts with `T:LinqToDB.`, `M:LinqToDB.`, `P:LinqToDB.`, `F:LinqToDB.`, or
`E:LinqToDB.`. It must exclude `LinqToDB.Internal.*` and external or compatibility members such as
`System.*`, `Microsoft.*`, `JetBrains.*`, or `BitOperations` as API entries. Those names may still
appear inside LinqToDB member signatures as parameter or return types.

Each extract entry should prefer this shape:

```md
### TypeName.MemberName

Search anchors: provider term, SQL term, common user wording, AI-Tags terms.
Namespace: LinqToDB...
Member kind: extension method / method / type / property / attribute.
Primary receivers: ITable<T>, IDataContext, ...
Returns: ...
AI-Tags: ...

Overloads:
| Form | Receiver | Required arguments | Optional arguments | Returns | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|

Remarks:
- Important XML-doc behavior that changes how code should be written.
- Version/provider/configuration constraints.
```

### Overload Policy

Do not paste every overload as a separate long section when overloads differ only by optional
parameters, sync/async shape, expression delegate type, cancellation token, or provider marker type.
Group them under one API family entry and list their forms in an overload table.

The extract should include enough overload information for discovery:

- receiver type, especially for extension methods;
- required arguments;
- meaningful optional arguments;
- return type;
- async vs sync distinction;
- provider-specific receiver or marker type;
- important generic constraints when XML-doc exposes them.

If the exact overload needed by the task is not shown in the compact extract, this does not mean it
is absent. Continue to `lib/<TFM>/linq2db.xml` and search the API family, member name, receiver
type, and parameter names before falling back to a generic API.

### Generic Parameter Policy

Generic parameters are important for exact C# code, but too much generic noise hurts retrieval.

Use this rule:

- keep generic arity or common generic names in signatures and overload tables;
- include generic receiver types such as `ITable<TSource>` or `IQueryable<T>` because they define
  where an extension method can be called;
- avoid putting long generic parameter lists in retrieval anchors unless the generic name is the
  thing users search for;
- do not create separate extract entries only because generic parameter names differ;
- verify exact generic parameters and constraints in XML-doc before writing final code when they
  matter.

Example only; not an actual API entry:

```text
### ProviderHints.<real typed helper from XML-doc>

| `<real helper signature from XML-doc>` | `<provider-specific receiver>` | `<required arguments>` | `<optional arguments>` | `<return type>` | `<XML-doc summary>` |
```

Avoid using the generic-heavy signature as the only retrieval anchor. Agents should be able to find
the entry by provider name, SQL hint text, helper name, receiver scope, and `Group=Hints`.

---

## Search Strategy

Search specific surfaces first, then broaden only when no match is found.

Ordering is part of the documentation contract. If a guide or extract lists a typed/provider-specific
path before a generic API, custom SQL, raw SQL, or interceptor path, treat the earlier path as the
preferred path to verify first.

| Task | Search for |
|---|---|
| Provider-specific API | Provider namespace, provider marker (`AsSqlServer`, `AsClickHouse`, etc.), provider `*Hints`/`*Tools`/extension type names |
| SQL hints | SQL hint text, `Group=Hints`, `HintType=...`, provider `*Hints` type, and `docs/hints-api-map.md` |
| Configuration | `DataOptions`, `UseXxx`, option name, provider name |
| Mapping | Attribute name, fluent method name, `MappingSchema`, `Configuration`, `DataType`, `DbType` |
| DML/query extension | Operation name, extension method name, `Group=DML`, `Group=Merge`, receiver type |
| Async API | Method name plus `Async`; remember async query/DML methods often require `using LinqToDB.Async;` |

When searching XML-doc, include both the user-facing SQL/database term and likely LinqToDB naming.
Provider-specific helpers do not always map one-to-one to SQL spelling.

---

## Fallbacks

Generic APIs are valid tools, but they are not proof that a typed API is absent.

Do not stop API discovery at a generic method just because it is documented. Generic methods are
fallbacks after typed/provider-specific lookup.

Before recommending these APIs, search XML-doc for a typed or provider-specific member:

- `QueryHint(...)`
- `TableHint(...)`
- `Sql.Expression`
- `Sql.Extension`
- `Sql.Table`
- raw SQL command text
- SQL text rewriting through interceptors

Use them when the typed API is missing, too narrow, or intentionally not available for the
requested provider feature.

---

## AI-Tags

Some XML-doc remarks contain `AI-Tags`. Use them as machine-readable classification, not as a
replacement for the full member documentation.

For hints:

- `HintType` tells where the hint is applied (`Table`, `TablesInScope`, `Join`, `Query`, etc.).
- When a provider-specific `Table` hint exists, also search for a matching `TablesInScope` hint for
  the same provider and SQL hint text before answering.
- For "several tables", "all tables", "whole query", or "scope" requests, search
  `HintType=TablesInScope` and `*InScope*` provider helpers before generic
  `TablesInScopeHint(...)`. Apply the scope helper to the query/subquery that already contains the
  target table references; applying it to only the first `ITable<T>` before adding joins affects
  only that source.
- Treat method-name patterns as search hints only. Common shapes are `<Base>Hint` ->
  `<Base>InScopeHint` and `With<Base>` -> `With<Base>InScope`. Verify the exact name in
  `docs/hints-api-map.md` and XML-doc; do not invent scope-helper names by string concatenation.
- The concrete SQL hint text is documented in the member XML summary, usually inside `<c>...</c>`.
- `docs/hints-api-map.md` is a reverse lookup built from that XML-doc shape. Use it to find
  candidate typed helpers, then verify exact signatures in XML-doc.
- The receiver type matters. A table hint helper and a query/scope hint helper can have similar
  names but different extension receiver types.

Missing from this compact section is not proof that an API or overload is absent. Search XML-doc before falling back to generic APIs.

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.CompareNulls

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CompareNulls, Compare, Nulls, NULL, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.CompareNulls` | If set to `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr` nullable fields would be checked for `IS NULL` in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql` comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSqlExceptParameters` is a backward compatible option that works mostly as `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql`, but sniffs parameters value and changes = into `IS NULL` when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into `IS NULL`. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ConcatenateOrderBy

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ConcatenateOrderBy, Concatenate, Order, LINQ, ORDER, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, API.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ConcatenateOrderBy` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderBy` or `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderByDescending` calls: if `true` - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call;if `false` - OrderBy* call will place sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls, after current call. Default value: `false`. Example: ` query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // =true - non-first OrderBy* calls treated as ordering continuation (non-standard) ORDER BY p.FIRST_NAME, p.LAST_NAME // =false - last OrderBy* calls have higher priority like they should per API documentation ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME, p.FIRST_NAME // to completely discard old sort specification, use API: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).RemoveOrderBy().OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // for any value will produce ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME ` |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DisableQueryCache

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DisableQueryCache, Disable, Query, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DisableQueryCache` | Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: `false`. It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use `LinqToDB.Linq.NoLinqCache` scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DoNotClearOrderBys

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DoNotClearOrderBys, Not, Clear, Order, Bys, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.DoNotClearOrderBys` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderBy` or `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderByDescending` calls: - if `true` - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call; - if `false` - OrderBy* call will discard sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.EnableContextSchemaEdit

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: EnableContextSchemaEdit, Enable, Context, Schema, Edit.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.EnableContextSchemaEdit` | If `true`, user could add new mappings to context mapping schems ( `LinqToDB.IDataContext.MappingSchema` ). Otherwise `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema` instance once, configure mappings for it and use this `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema` instance for all context instances. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.GenerateExpressionTest

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: GenerateExpressionTest, Generate, Expression, Test, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.GenerateExpressionTest` | Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to `linq2db` subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch` for more details. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.GuardGrouping

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: GuardGrouping, Guard, Grouping, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.GuardGrouping` | Controls behavior of LINQ query, which ends with GroupBy call. - if `true` - `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be thrown for such queries; - if `false` - eager loading used. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.IgnoreEmptyUpdate

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IgnoreEmptyUpdate, Ignore, Empty, Update.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.IgnoreEmptyUpdate` | Controls behavior of linq2db when there is no updateable fields in Update query: - if `true` - query not executed and Update operation returns 0 as number of affected records; - if `false` - `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be thrown. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.KeepDistinctOrdered

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: KeepDistinctOrdered, Keep, Distinct, Ordered, SQL, SELECT, DISTINCT, FROM, ORDER, GROUP.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.KeepDistinctOrdered` | Allows SQL generation to automatically transform `SELECT DISTINCT value FROM Table ORDER BY date` Into GROUP BY equivalent if syntax is not supported Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.OptimizeJoins

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: OptimizeJoins, Optimize, Joins, SQL, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.OptimizeJoins` | If enabled, linq2db will try to reduce number of generated SQL JOINs for LINQ query. Attempted optimizations: - removes duplicate joins by unique target table key; - removes self-joins by unique key; - removes left joins if joined table is not used in query. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.Options

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Options.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.Options` | Default `LinqToDB.LinqOptions` options. Automatically synchronized with other settings in `LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq` class. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ParameterizeTakeSkip

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ParameterizeTakeSkip, Parameterize, Take, Skip.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.ParameterizeTakeSkip` | Enables Take/Skip parameterization. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.PreferApply

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: PreferApply, Prefer, Apply, CROSS, APPLY, OUTER.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.PreferApply` | Used to generate CROSS APPLY or OUTER APPLY if possible. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.PreloadGroups

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: PreloadGroups, Preload, Groups, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.PreloadGroups` | Controls how group data for LINQ queries ended with GroupBy will be loaded: - if `true` - group data will be loaded together with main query, resulting in 1 + N queries, where N - number of groups; - if `false` - group data will be loaded when you call enumerator for specific group `System.Linq.IGrouping`. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.TraceMapperExpression

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: TraceMapperExpression, Trace, Mapper, Expression.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Linq.TraceMapperExpression` | Enables logging of generated mapping expression to data connection tracing infrastructure. See `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch` for more details. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: LinqService, Linq, Service.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService` | Remote context global settings. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService.SerializeAssemblyQualifiedName

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: SerializeAssemblyQualifiedName, Serialize, Assembly, Qualified, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService.SerializeAssemblyQualifiedName` | Controls format of type name, sent over remote context: - if `true` - name from `System.Type.AssemblyQualifiedName` used; - if `false` - name from `System.Type.FullName` used. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService.ThrowUnresolvedTypeException

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: ThrowUnresolvedTypeException, Throw, Unresolved, Type, Exception.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.LinqService.ThrowUnresolvedTypeException` | Controls behavior of Linq To DB, when it cannot load `System.Type` by type name on query deserialization: - if `true` - `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be thrown; - if `false` - type load error will be ignored. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxArrayParameterLengthLogging

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: MaxArrayParameterLengthLogging, Max, Array, Parameter, Length, Logging, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxArrayParameterLengthLogging` | Determines number of items after which logging of collection data in SQL will be truncated. This is to avoid Out-Of-Memory exceptions when getting SqlText from `LinqToDB.Data.TraceInfo`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxBinaryParameterLengthLogging

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: MaxBinaryParameterLengthLogging, Max, Binary, Parameter, Length, Logging, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxBinaryParameterLengthLogging` | Determines the length after which logging of binary data in SQL will be truncated. This is to avoid Out-Of-Memory exceptions when getting SqlText from `LinqToDB.Data.TraceInfo`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxStringParameterLengthLogging

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: MaxStringParameterLengthLogging, Max, String, Parameter, Length, Logging, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.MaxStringParameterLengthLogging` | Determines the length after which logging of string data in SQL will be truncated. This is to avoid Out-Of-Memory exceptions when getting SqlText from `LinqToDB.Data.TraceInfo`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.OptimizeForSequentialAccess

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: OptimizeForSequentialAccess, Optimize, For, Sequential, Access.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.OptimizeForSequentialAccess` | Enables mapping expression to be compatible with `System.Data.CommandBehavior.SequentialAccess` behavior. Note that it doesn't switch linq2db to use `System.Data.CommandBehavior.SequentialAccess` behavior for queries, so this optimization could be used for `System.Data.CommandBehavior.Default` too. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultCoefficient

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DefaultCoefficient, Default, Coefficient.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultCoefficient` | The default coefficient for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must be nonnegative. Default value: 1 second. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultExponentialBase

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DefaultExponentialBase, Default, Exponential, Base.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultExponentialBase` | The default base for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must not be lesser than 1. Default value: `2`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultMaxDelay

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DefaultMaxDelay, Default, Max, Delay.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultMaxDelay` | The default maximum time delay between retries, must be nonnegative. Default value: 30 seconds. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultMaxRetryCount

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DefaultMaxRetryCount, Default, Max, Retry, Count.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultMaxRetryCount` | The default number of retry attempts. Default value: `5`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultRandomFactor

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DefaultRandomFactor, Default, Random, Factor.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.DefaultRandomFactor` | The default maximum random factor, must not be lesser than 1. Default value: `1.1`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.Factory

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Factory.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.Factory` | Retry policy factory method, used to create retry policy for new `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection` instance. If factory method is not set, retry policy is not used. Not set by default. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.Options

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Options.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.Options` | Default `LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions` options. Automatically synchronized with other settings in `LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy` class. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.UseDefaultPolicy

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: UseDefaultPolicy, Use, Default, Policy.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.UseDefaultPolicy` | Status of use of default retry policy. Getter returns `true` if default retry policy used, and false if custom retry policy used or retry policy is not set. Setter sets `LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.RetryPolicy.Factory` to default retry policy factory if `value` is `true`, otherwise removes retry policy. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: Sql, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql` | SQL generation global settings. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.AssociationAlias

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: AssociationAlias, Association, Alias, SQL, SELECT, FROM, LEFT, JOIN.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.AssociationAlias` | Format for association alias. Default value: `"a_{0}"`. In the following query ` var query = from child in db.Child select new { child.ChildID, child.Parent.Value1 }; ` for association `Parent` will be generated association `A_Parent` in resulting SQL. ` SELECT \[child\].\[ChildID\], \[a_Parent\].\[Value1\] FROM \[Child\] \[child\] LEFT JOIN \[Parent\] \[a_Parent\] ON (\[child\].\[ParentID\] = \[a_Parent\].\[ParentID\]) ` Set this value to `null` to disable special alias generation queries. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy, Enable, Constant, Expression, Order, ORDER.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.EnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy` | If `true`, linq2db will allow any constant expressions in ORDER BY clause. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.GenerateFinalAliases

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: GenerateFinalAliases, Generate, Final, Aliases, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.GenerateFinalAliases` | Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: `false`. For the query ` var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; ` When property is `true` ` SELECT \[child\].\[ChildID\] as \[TrackId\] FROM \[Child\] \[child\] ` Otherwise alias will be removed ` SELECT \[child\].\[ChildID\] FROM \[Child\] \[child\] ` |  |

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Options.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql.Options` | Default `LinqToDB.SqlOptions` options. Automatically synchronized with other settings in `LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.Sql` class. |  |

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: SqlServer, Sql, Server.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.SqlServer` | SqlServer specific global settings. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.SqlServer.UseSchemaOnlyToGetSchema

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: UseSchemaOnlyToGetSchema, Use, Schema, Only, Get.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.SqlServer.UseSchemaOnlyToGetSchema` | if set to `true`, SchemaProvider uses `System.Data.CommandBehavior.SchemaOnly` to get metadata. Otherwise, the `sp_describe_first_result_set` sproc is used. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.TraceMaterializationActivity

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: TraceMaterializationActivity, Trace, Materialization, Activity.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.TraceMaterializationActivity` | Enables tracing of object materialization activity. It can significantly break performance if tracing consumer performs slow, so it is disabled by default. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.TranslationThreadMaxHopCount

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: TranslationThreadMaxHopCount, Translation, Thread, Max, Hop, Count.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.TranslationThreadMaxHopCount` | Maximum number of hops to other threads during translation. When all threads used up, application will throw `System.InsufficientExecutionStackException`. To disable this feature and produce `System.StackOverflowException` when stack is used up, set -1 as value. Default value: 5. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.UseEnumValueNameForStringColumns

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: UseEnumValueNameForStringColumns, Use, Enum, Value, Name, For, String, Columns.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.UseEnumValueNameForStringColumns` | If `true` - Enum values are stored as by calling ToString(). Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.UseNullableTypesMetadata

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: UseNullableTypesMetadata, Use, Nullable, Types, Metadata.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.UseNullableTypesMetadata` | Whether or not Nullable Reference Types annotations from C# are read and taken into consideration to determine if a column or association can be null. Nullable Types can be overriden with explicit CanBeNull annotations in \[Column\], \[Association\], or \[Nullable\]. |  |

Search anchors: Invoke.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.CompiledQuery.Invoke``2(``0)` | Executes compiled query against provided database connection context. |  |

| `M:LinqToDB.CompiledQuery.Invoke``4(``0,``1,``2)` | Executes compiled query with two parameters against provided database connection context. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.CompiledQuery.Invoke``5(``0,``1,``2,``3)` | Executes compiled query with three parameters against provided database connection context. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.CompiledQuery.Invoke``6(``0,``1,``2,``3,``4)` | Executes compiled query with four parameters against provided database connection context. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.CompiledQuery.Invoke``7(``0,``1,``2,``3,``4,``5)` | Executes compiled query with five parameters against provided database connection context. |  |

SAP HANA: assemblyName - SAP HANA provider assembly name. Oracle: assemblyName - Oracle provider assembly name. SQL Server: assemblyName - provider assembly name. Recognized values: `"System.Data"`, `"System.Data.SqlClient"`, `"Microsoft.Data.SqlClient"`. version - T-SQL support level, recognized values: `"2005"`, `"2008"`, `"2012"`, `"2014"`, `"2016"`, `"2017"`, `"2018"`. Default: `"2008"`. DB2: version - DB2 platform, recognized values: `"zOS"` or `"z/OS"` - DB2 for z/OS. Default platform - DB2 LUW. |  |

### LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.DatabaseName

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DatabaseName, Database, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

### LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.DefaultNullable

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DefaultNullable, Default, Nullable, NOT, NULL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.CreateTableOptions.DefaultNullable` | Defines how columns nullability flag should be generated: - `LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.Null` - generate only `NOT NULL` for non-nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as `NULL` by database. - `LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.NotNull` - generate only `NULL` for nullable fields. Missing nullability information treated as `NOT NULL` by database. - `LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.None` - explicitly generate `NULL` and `NOT NULL` for all columns. Default value: `LinqToDB.SqlQuery.DefaultNullable.None`. |  |

Search anchors: SchemaName, Schema, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

Recognized by PostgreSQL 12+, SQLite 3.35+, and ClickHouse 26.3+. Other providers silently drop the hint without tracing — if you rely on the hint for performance, be aware it is a no-op on those providers. |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.KeepIdentity

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: KeepIdentity, Keep, Identity.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.KeepIdentity` | If this option set to true, bulk copy will use values of columns, marked with IsIdentity flag. SkipOnInsert flag in this case will be ignored. Otherwise those columns will be skipped and values will be generated by server. Not compatible with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.RowByRow` mode. |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.KeepNulls

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: KeepNulls, Keep, Nulls, NULL, SQL, ASE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.KeepNulls` | Enables instert of `NULL` values instead of values from colum default constraint during bulk copy operation. Supported with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.ProviderSpecific` bulk copy mode for following databases: SQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.NotifyAfter

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: NotifyAfter, Notify, After.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.NotifyAfter` | Gets or sets counter after how many copied records `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.RowsCopiedCallback` should be called. E.g. if you set it to 10, callback will be called after each 10 copied records. To disable callback, set this option to 0 (default value). |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.RowsCopiedCallback

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.UseInternalTransaction

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: UseInternalTransaction, Use, Internal, Transaction, ASE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.UseInternalTransaction` | Enables automatic transaction creation during bulk copy operation. Supported with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.ProviderSpecific` bulk copy mode for following databases: OracleSQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.UseParameters

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: UseParameters, Use, Parameters.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.UseParameters` | Gets or sets whether to always use Parameters for MultipleRowsCopy. Default is false. If True, provider-specific parameter limit per batch will be used to determine the maximum number of rows per insert, Unless overridden by `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.MaxParametersForBatch`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.WithoutSession

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: WithoutSession, Without, Session.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.WithoutSession` | Implemented only by ClickHouse.Driver provider. When set, provider-specific bulk copy will use session-less connection even if called over connection with session. Note that session-less connections cannot be used with session-bound functionality like temporary tables. |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.MultipleRows

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: MultipleRows, Multiple, Rows, INSERT, FROM, SELECT.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.MultipleRows` | Data will be inserted into table as a batch insert using INSERT FROM SELECT or similar code. If method not supported, it will be downgraded to `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.RowByRow` method. |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.ProviderSpecific

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: ProviderSpecific, Provider, Specific.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.ProviderSpecific` | Data will be inserted using native bulk copy functionality if supported. If method not supported, it will be downgraded to `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.RowByRow` method. |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.RowByRow

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: RowByRow, Row.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.RowByRow` | Data will be inserted into table as a sequence of selects, row by row. |  |

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: ConflictAction, Conflict, Action.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction` | Defines the action to take when conflicts occur during bulk copy operations. |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction.Default

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Default.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction.Default` | Default behavior - conflicts will be handled according to the database's normal behavior (typically will fail). |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction.Ignore

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Ignore, INSERT, IGNORE, INTO, CONFLICT, NOTHING.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Data.ConflictAction.Ignore` | Ignore conflicts during bulk copy operation. Supported for following databases: MySql/MariaDB - uses INSERT IGNORE INTO statement (only works with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.MultipleRows` )PostgreSQL - adds ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING clause to INSERT INTO statement (only works with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.MultipleRows` )SQLite - uses INSERT OR IGNORE INTO statement (only works with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.MultipleRows` ) |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.BeginTransaction` | Starts new transaction for current connection with default isolation level. | `M:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.BeginTransaction(System.Data.IsolationLevel)` | Starts new transaction for current connection with specified isolation level. If connection already has transaction, it will throw `System.InvalidOperationException`. | Group=Connection; Execution=Immediate; Composability=Terminal; Pipeline=SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined; |

### LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.CommandTimeout

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CommandTimeout, Command, Timeout.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.CommandTimeout` | Gets or sets command execution timeout in seconds. Supported values: 0: infinite timeout > 0: command timeout in seconds -1 on property get: default provider/connection command timeout value used (not controlled by Linq To DB) negative value on property set: throws `System.InvalidOperationException` exception. To reset timeout to provider/connection defaults use `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.ResetCommandTimeout` or `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.ResetCommandTimeoutAsync` methods |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: TraceSwitch, Trace, Switch.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch` | Gets or sets global data connection trace options. Used for all new connections unless `LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseTraceLevel` is called on builder. defaults to off unless library was built in debug mode. Should only be used when `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitchConnection` can not be used! |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitchConnection

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: TraceSwitchConnection, Trace, Switch, Connection.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitchConnection` | gets or sets the trace switch, this is used by some methods to determine if `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.OnTraceConnection` should be called. defaults to `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch` used for current connection instance. |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.WriteTraceLine

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: WriteTraceLine, Write, Trace, Line.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.WriteTraceLine` | Trace function. By Default use `System.Diagnostics.Debug` class for logging, but could be replaced to log e.g. to your log file. will be ignored if `LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseTraceWith` is called on builder First parameter contains trace message. Second parameter contains trace message category ( `System.Diagnostics.Switch.DisplayName` ). Third parameter contains trace level for message ( `System.Diagnostics.TraceLevel` ). `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch` Should only not use to write trace lines, only use `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.WriteTraceLineConnection`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.WriteTraceLineConnection

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|

### LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSql

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: FromSql, From, Sql, LINQ, SQL, SELECT, FROM, API, Group=Helpers, Execution=Deferred, Composability=Composable, Affects=QueryRoot.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSql``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,LinqToDB.RawSqlString,System.Object[])` | Creates a LINQ query based on a raw SQL query. If the database provider supports composing on the supplied SQL, you can compose on top of the raw SQL query using LINQ operators - `context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM dbo.Blogs").OrderBy(b => b.Name);` As with any API that accepts SQL it is important to parameterize any user input to protect against a SQL injection attack. You can include parameter place holders in the SQL query string and then supply parameter values as additional arguments. Any parameter values you supply will automatically be converted to a DbParameter - `context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM \[dbo\].\[SearchBlogs\]({0})", userSuppliedSearchTerm);` This overload also accepts DbParameter instances as parameter values. `context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM \[dbo\].\[SearchBlogs\]({0})", new DataParameter("@searchTerm", userSuppliedSearchTerm, DataType.Int64));` | Group=Helpers; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=QueryRoot; |
| `M:LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSql``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.FormattableString)` | Creates a LINQ query based on an interpolated string representing a SQL query. If the database provider supports composing on the supplied SQL, you can compose on top of the raw SQL query using LINQ operators - `context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM dbo.Blogs").OrderBy(b => b.Name);` As with any API that accepts SQL it is important to parameterize any user input to protect against a SQL injection attack. You can include interpolated parameter place holders in the SQL query string. Any interpolated parameter values you supply will automatically be converted to a DbParameter - `context.FromSql<Blogs>($"SELECT * FROM \[dbo\].\[SearchBlogs\]({userSuppliedSearchTerm})");` | Group=Helpers; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=QueryRoot; |

### LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSqlScalar

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: FromSqlScalar, From, Sql, Scalar, LINQ, SQL, SELECT, FROM, API, UNNEST, Group=Helpers, Execution=Deferred, Composability=Composable, Affects=QueryRoot.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataExtensions.FromSqlScalar``1(LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.FormattableString)` | Creates a LINQ query based on an interpolated string representing a SQL query. If the database provider supports composing on the supplied SQL, you can compose on top of the raw SQL query using LINQ operators - `context.FromSql<Blogs>("SELECT * FROM dbo.Blogs").OrderBy(b => b.Name);` As with any API that accepts SQL it is important to parameterize any user input to protect against a SQL injection attack. You can include interpolated parameter place holders in the SQL query string. Any interpolated parameter values you supply will automatically be converted to a DbParameter - `context.FromSqlScalar<Blogs>($"UNNEST({array})");` An alias for the scalar value will be generated automatically with the name `value`. For most databases, the following SQL will be generated: `SELECT t.value FROM (SELECT '1') AS t(value)` For databases that do not support this syntax, ensure that your query provides an alias for the column named `value` to allow correct translation, for example: `SELECT t.value FROM (SELECT '1' AS value) AS t` | Group=Helpers; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=QueryRoot; |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyDatabaseName(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.String)` | Gets or sets explicit name of target database instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. ### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyFireTriggers

Kind: Method.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyFireTriggers(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})` | Enables insert triggers during bulk copy operation. Supported with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.ProviderSpecific` bulk copy mode for following databases: OracleSQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyKeepIdentity

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseBulkCopyKeepIdentity, Use, Bulk, Copy, Keep, Identity.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyKeepIdentity(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})` | If this option set to true, bulk copy will use values of columns, marked with IsIdentity flag. SkipOnInsert flag in this case will be ignored. Otherwise those columns will be skipped and values will be generated by server. Not compatible with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.RowByRow` mode. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyKeepNulls

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseBulkCopyKeepNulls, Use, Bulk, Copy, Keep, Nulls, NULL, SQL, ASE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyKeepNulls(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})` | Enables instert of `NULL` values instead of values from colum default constraint during bulk copy operation. Supported with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.ProviderSpecific` bulk copy mode for following databases: SQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASE |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyMaxBatchSize

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseBulkCopyMaxBatchSize, Use, Bulk, Copy, Max, Batch, Size.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyMaxBatchSize(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Nullable{System.Int32})` | Number of rows in each batch. At the end of each batch, the rows in the batch are sent to the server. Returns an integer value or zero if no value has been set. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyMaxDegreeOfParallelism

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyMaxDegreeOfParallelism(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Nullable{System.Int32})` | Implemented only by ClickHouse.Driver provider. Defines number of connections, used for parallel insert in `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.ProviderSpecific` mode. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyMaxParametersForBatch

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyMaxParametersForBatch(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Nullable{System.Int32})` | If set, will set the maximum parameters per batch statement. Also see `LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyUseParameters`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyNotifyAfter

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseBulkCopyNotifyAfter, Use, Bulk, Copy, Notify, After.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyNotifyAfter(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Int32)` | Gets or sets counter after how many copied records `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.RowsCopiedCallback` should be called. E.g. if you set it to 10, callback will be called after each 10 copied records. To disable callback, set this option to 0 (default value). |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyRowsCopiedCallback

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopyRowsCopiedCallback(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Action{LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyRowsCopied})` | Gets or sets callback method that will be called by BulkCopy operation after each `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.NotifyAfter` rows copied. This callback will not be used if `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.NotifyAfter` set to 0. |  |

| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseBulkCopySchemaName(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.String)` | Gets or sets explicit name of target schema/owner instead of one, configured for copied entity in mapping schema. Note that session-less connections cannot be used with session-bound functionality like temporary tables. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseCompareNulls

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseCompareNulls, Use, Compare, Nulls, NULL, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseCompareNulls(LinqToDB.DataOptions,LinqToDB.CompareNulls)` | If set to `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr` nullable fields would be checked for `IS NULL` in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql` comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSqlExceptParameters` is a backward compatible option that works mostly as `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql`, but sniffs parameters value and changes = into `IS NULL` when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into `IS NULL`. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseConcatenateOrderBy

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseConcatenateOrderBy, Use, Concatenate, Order, LINQ, ORDER, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, API.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseConcatenateOrderBy(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderBy` or `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderByDescending` calls: if `true` - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call;if `false` - OrderBy* call will place sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls, after current call. Default value: `false`. Example: ` query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // =true - non-first OrderBy* calls treated as ordering continuation (non-standard) ORDER BY p.FIRST_NAME, p.LAST_NAME // =false - last OrderBy* calls have higher priority like they should per API documentation ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME, p.FIRST_NAME // to completely discard old sort specification, use API: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).RemoveOrderBy().OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // for any value will produce ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME ` |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseDisableQueryCache

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseDisableQueryCache, Use, Disable, Query, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseDisableQueryCache(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: `false`. It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use `LinqToDB.Linq.NoLinqCache` scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseDoNotClearOrderBys

Search anchors: UseDoNotClearOrderBys, Use, Not, Clear, Order, Bys, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseDoNotClearOrderBys(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderBy` or `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderByDescending` calls: - if `true` - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call; - if `false` - OrderBy* call will discard sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseEnableContextSchemaEdit

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseEnableContextSchemaEdit, Use, Enable, Context, Schema, Edit.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseEnableContextSchemaEdit(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | If `true`, user could add new mappings to context mapping schems ( `LinqToDB.IDataContext.MappingSchema` ). Otherwise, `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema` instance once, configure mappings for it and use this `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema` instance for all context instances. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGenerateExpressionTest

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseGenerateExpressionTest, Use, Generate, Expression, Test, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGenerateExpressionTest(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to `linq2db` subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch` for more details. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGenerateFinalAliases

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseGenerateFinalAliases, Use, Generate, Final, Aliases, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGenerateFinalAliases(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: `false`. For the query ` var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; ` When property is `true` ` SELECT \[child\].\[ChildID\] as \[TrackId\] FROM \[Child\] \[child\] ` Otherwise alias will be removed ` SELECT \[child\].\[ChildID\] FROM \[Child\] \[child\] ` |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGuardGrouping

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseGuardGrouping, Use, Guard, Grouping, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseGuardGrouping(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior of LINQ query, which ends with GroupBy call. - if `true` - `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be thrown for such queries; - if `false` - eager loading used. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseIgnoreEmptyUpdate

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseIgnoreEmptyUpdate, Use, Ignore, Empty, Update.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseIgnoreEmptyUpdate(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior of `linq2db` when there is no updateable fields in Update query: - if `true` - query not executed and Update operation returns 0 as number of affected records; - if `false` - `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be thrown. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptor

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseInterceptor, Use, Interceptor.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptor(LinqToDB.DataOptions,LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor)` | Adds `LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor` instance to those registered on the context. Interceptors can observe or modify supported LinqToDB operation stages. Command execution interceptors can suppress provider execution when their contract returns an explicit result. See the specific implementations of `LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor` for details. For example, 'ICommandInterceptor'. Extensions can also register multiple `LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor` s in the internal service provider. If both injected and application interceptors are found, then the injected interceptors are run in the order that they are resolved from the service provider, and then the application interceptors are run in the order that they were added to the context. Calling this method multiple times will result in all interceptors in every call being added to the context. Interceptors added in a previous call are not overridden by interceptors added in a later call. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptors

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseInterceptors, Use, Interceptors.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptors(LinqToDB.DataOptions,LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor[])` | Adds `LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor` instances to those registered on the context. Interceptors can observe or modify supported LinqToDB operation stages. Command execution interceptors can suppress provider execution when their contract returns an explicit result. See the specific implementations of `LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor` for details. For example, 'ICommandInterceptor'. Extensions can also register multiple `LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor` s in the internal service provider. If both injected and application interceptors are found, then the injected interceptors are run in the order that they are resolved from the service provider, and then the application interceptors are run in the order that they were added to the context. Calling this method multiple times will result in all interceptors in every call being added to the context. Interceptors added in a previous call are not overridden by interceptors added in a later call. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseInterceptors(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor})` | Adds `LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor` instances to those registered on the context. Interceptors can observe or modify supported LinqToDB operation stages. Command execution interceptors can suppress provider execution when their contract returns an explicit result. See the specific implementations of `LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor` for details. For example, 'ICommandInterceptor'. A single interceptor instance can implement multiple different interceptor interfaces. It will be registered as an interceptor for all interfaces that it implements. Extensions can also register multiple `LinqToDB.Interceptors.IInterceptor` s in the internal service provider. If both injected and application interceptors are found, then the injected interceptors are run in the order that they are resolved from the service provider, and then the application interceptors are run in the order that they were added to the context. Calling this method multiple times will result in all interceptors in every call being added to the context. Interceptors added in a previous call are not overridden by interceptors added in a later call. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseMemberTranslator

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UseMemberTranslator, Use, Member, Translator, NET, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseMemberTranslator(LinqToDB.DataOptions,LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.IMemberTranslator)` | Adds `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.IMemberTranslator` instance to those registered on the context. Translators can be used to translate.NET member expressions into SQL expressions during query translation. This is the low-level extensibility point behind provider or application-specific method translation. Prefer `\[Sql.Expression\]`, `\[Sql.Function\]`, or `\[ExpressionMethod\]` for simpler cases; use `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.IMemberTranslator` when you need direct access to translation context and SQL-expression creation. See package-local `skills/linq2db/docs/custom-sql.md` and `skills/linq2db/docs/configuration.md` for guidance on choosing between these extension points. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UseMemberTranslator(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.IMemberTranslator})` | Adds collection `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.IMemberTranslator` instance to those registered on the context. Translators can be used to translate.NET member expressions into SQL expressions during query translation. Registration order matters when multiple translators can handle the same member. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UsePreferExistsForScalar

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UsePreferExistsForScalar, Use, Prefer, Exists, For, Scalar, SQL, EXISTS, SELECT, FROM, WHERE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UsePreferExistsForScalar(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Depending on this option `linq2db` generates different SQL for `sequence.Contains(value)`. `true` - `EXISTS (SELECT * FROM sequence WHERE sequence.key = value)`. `false` - `value IN (SELECT sequence.key FROM sequence)`. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UsePreloadGroups

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UsePreloadGroups, Use, Preload, Groups, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.UsePreloadGroups(LinqToDB.DataOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls how group data for LINQ queries ended with GroupBy will be loaded: - if `true` - group data will be loaded together with main query, resulting in 1 + N queries, where N - number of groups; - if `false` - group data will be loaded when you call enumerator for specific group `System.Linq.IGrouping`. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithCompareNulls

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithCompareNulls, With, Compare, Nulls, NULL, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithCompareNulls(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,LinqToDB.CompareNulls)` | If set to `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr` nullable fields would be checked for `IS NULL` in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql` comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSqlExceptParameters` is a backward compatible option that works mostly as `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql`, but sniffs parameters value and changes = into `IS NULL` when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into `IS NULL`. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithConcatenateOrderBy

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithConcatenateOrderBy, With, Concatenate, Order, LINQ, ORDER, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, API.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithConcatenateOrderBy(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderBy` or `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderByDescending` calls: if `true` - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call;if `false` - OrderBy* call will place sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls, after current call. Default value: `false`. Example: ` query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // =true - non-first OrderBy* calls treated as ordering continuation (non-standard) ORDER BY p.FIRST_NAME, p.LAST_NAME // =false - last OrderBy* calls have higher priority like they should per API documentation ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME, p.FIRST_NAME // to completely discard old sort specification, use API: query.OrderBy(r => r.FirstName).RemoveOrderBy().OrderBy(r => r.LastName) // for any value will produce ORDER BY p.LAST_NAME ` |  |

| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDataProviderFactory(LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,System.Func{LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider})` | Sets DataProviderFactory option. |  |

Kind: Method.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDbTransaction(LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,System.Data.Common.DbTransaction)` | Sets DbTransaction option. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisableQueryCache

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithDisableQueryCache, With, Disable, Query, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisableQueryCache(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: `false`. It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use `LinqToDB.Linq.NoLinqCache` scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisposeConnection

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithDisposeConnection, With, Dispose, Connection.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDisposeConnection(LinqToDB.Data.ConnectionOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})` | Sets DisposeConnection option. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDoNotClearOrderBys

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithDoNotClearOrderBys, With, Not, Clear, Order, Bys, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithDoNotClearOrderBys(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderBy` or `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderByDescending` calls: - if `true` - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call; - if `false` - OrderBy* call will discard sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithEnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithEnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy, With, Enable, Constant, Expression, Order, ORDER.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithEnableConstantExpressionInOrderBy(LinqToDB.SqlOptions,System.Boolean)` | If `true`, linq2db will allow any constant expressions in ORDER BY clause. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithEnableContextSchemaEdit

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithEnableContextSchemaEdit, With, Enable, Context, Schema, Edit.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithEnableContextSchemaEdit(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | If `true`, user could add new mappings to context mapping schems ( `LinqToDB.IDataContext.MappingSchema` ). Otherwise, `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema` instance once, configure mappings for it and use this `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema` instance for all context instances. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithExponentialBase

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithExponentialBase, With, Exponential, Base.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithExponentialBase(LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions,System.Double)` | The base for the exponential function used to compute the delay between retries, must not be lesser than 1. Default value: `2`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithFactory

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithFactory, With, Factory.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithFactory(LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.RetryPolicyOptions,System.Func{LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection,LinqToDB.Data.RetryPolicy.IRetryPolicy})` | Retry policy factory method, used to create retry policy for new `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection` instance. If factory method is not set, retry policy is not used. Not set by default. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithFireTriggers

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithFireTriggers, With, Fire, Triggers.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithFireTriggers(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})` | When specified, causes the server to fire the insert triggers for the rows being inserted into the database. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateExpressionTest

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithGenerateExpressionTest, With, Generate, Expression, Test, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateExpressionTest(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to `linq2db` subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch` for more details. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateFinalAliases

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithGenerateFinalAliases, With, Generate, Final, Aliases, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGenerateFinalAliases(LinqToDB.SqlOptions,System.Boolean)` | Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: `false`. For the query ` var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; ` When property is `true` ` SELECT \[child\].\[ChildID\] as \[TrackId\] FROM \[Child\] \[child\] ` Otherwise alias will be removed ` SELECT \[child\].\[ChildID\] FROM \[Child\] \[child\] ` |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGuardGrouping

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithGuardGrouping, With, Guard, Grouping, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithGuardGrouping(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior of LINQ query, which ends with GroupBy call. - if `true` - `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be thrown for such queries; - if `false` - eager loading used. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithIgnoreEmptyUpdate

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithIgnoreEmptyUpdate, With, Ignore, Empty, Update.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithIgnoreEmptyUpdate(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls behavior of `linq2db` when there is no updateable fields in Update query: - if `true` - query not executed and Update operation returns 0 as number of affected records; - if `false` - `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be thrown. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepDistinctOrdered

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithKeepDistinctOrdered, With, Keep, Distinct, Ordered, SQL, SELECT, DISTINCT, FROM, ORDER, GROUP.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepDistinctOrdered(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Allows SQL generation to automatically transform `SELECT DISTINCT value FROM Table ORDER BY date` Into GROUP BY equivalent if syntax is not supported Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepIdentity

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithKeepIdentity, With, Keep, Identity.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepIdentity(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})` | If this option set to true, bulk copy will use values of columns, marked with IsIdentity flag. SkipOnInsert flag in this case will be ignored. Otherwise, those columns will be skipped and values will be generated by server. Not compatible with `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyType.RowByRow` mode. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepNulls

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithKeepNulls(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Nullable{System.Boolean})` | Preserves null values in the destination table regardless of the settings for default values. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithNotifyAfter(LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions,System.Int32)` | Gets or sets counter after how many copied records `LinqToDB.Data.BulkCopyOptions.RowsCopiedCallback` should be called. E.g. if you set it to 10, callback will be called after each 10 copied records. To disable callback, set this option to 0 (default value). |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithOptimizeJoins

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithOptimizeJoins, With, Optimize, Joins, SQL, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithOptimizeJoins(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | If enabled, `linq2db` will try to reduce number of generated SQL JOINs for LINQ query. Attempted optimizations: - removes duplicate joins by unique target table key; - removes self-joins by unique key; - removes left joins if joined table is not used in query. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithParameterizeTakeSkip

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithParameterizeTakeSkip, With, Parameterize, Take, Skip.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithParameterizeTakeSkip(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Enables Take/Skip parameterization. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreferApply

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithPreferApply, With, Prefer, Apply, CROSS, APPLY, OUTER.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreferApply(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Used to generate CROSS APPLY or OUTER APPLY if possible. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreferExistsForScalar

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithPreferExistsForScalar, With, Prefer, Exists, For, Scalar, SQL, EXISTS, SELECT, FROM, WHERE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreferExistsForScalar(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Depending on this option `linq2db` generates different SQL for `sequence.Contains(value)`. `true` - `EXISTS (SELECT * FROM sequence WHERE sequence.key = value)`. `false` - `value IN (SELECT sequence.key FROM sequence)`. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreloadGroups

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: WithPreloadGroups, With, Preload, Groups, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataOptionsExtensions.WithPreloadGroups(LinqToDB.LinqOptions,System.Boolean)` | Controls how group data for LINQ queries ended with GroupBy will be loaded: - if `true` - group data will be loaded together with main query, resulting in 1 + N queries, where N - number of groups; - if `false` - group data will be loaded when you call enumerator for specific group `System.Linq.IGrouping`. Default value: `false`. |  |
