### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode, Firebird, Identifier, Quote, Mode.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode` | Possible modes for Firebird identifier quotes. This enumeration covers only identifier quotation logic and don't handle identifier length limits. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Auto

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Auto, LINQ, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Auto` | Quote identifiers if needed. LINQ To DB will quote identifiers, if they are not valid without quotation. This includes: - use of reserved words; - use of any characters except latin letters, digits, _ and $; - use digit, _ or $ as first character. This is default mode. Note that if you need to preserve casing of identifiers, you should use `LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Quote` mode. Quoted identifiers not supported by SQL Dialect < 1. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.None

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: None, LINQ, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.None` | Do not quote identifiers. LINQ To DB will not check identifiers for validity (spaces, reserved words) is this mode. This mode should be used only for SQL Dialect < 1 and it is developer's responsibility to ensure that there is no identifiers in use that require quotation. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Quote

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Quote, LINQ, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Firebird.FirebirdIdentifierQuoteMode.Quote` | Always quote identifiers. LINQ To DB will quote all identifiers, even if it is not required. Select this mode, if you need to preserve identifiers casing. Quoted identifiers not supported by SQL Dialect < 1. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.GetQueryParameterNormalizer

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: GetQueryParameterNormalizer, Get, Query, Parameter, Normalizer.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.IDataProvider.GetQueryParameterNormalizer` | Returns instance of `LinqToDB.Internal.DataProvider.IQueryParametersNormalizer`, which implements normalization logic for parameters of single query. E.g. it could include: trimming names that are too longremoving/replacing unsupported charactersname deduplication for parameters with same name. For implementation without state it is recommended to return static instance. E.g. this could be done for providers with positional parameters that ignore names. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertAll

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: InsertAll, Insert, All, INSERT, ALL, INTO, VALUES.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertAll` | This mode generates INSERT ALL statement. Note that INSERT ALL doesn't support sequences and will use single generated value for all rows. ` INSERT ALL INTO target_table VALUES(/*row data*/)... INTO target_table VALUES(/*row data*/) ` |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertDual

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: InsertDual, Insert, Dual, INSERT, SELECT, INTO, FROM, DUAL, UNION, ALL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertDual` | This mode generates INSERT... SELECT statement. ` INSERT INTO target_table(/*columns*/) SELECT /*row data*/ FROM DUAL UNION ALL... UNION ALL SELECT /*row data*/ FROM DUAL ` |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertInto

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: InsertInto, Insert, Into, INSERT, INTO, VALUES.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.Oracle.AlternativeBulkCopy.InsertInto` | This mode performs regular INSERT INTO query with array of values for each column. ` INSERT INTO target_table(/*columns*/) VALUES(:column1ArrayParameter,...,:columnXArrayParameter) ` |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

| `T:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode` | Identifier quotation logic for SQL generation. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.Auto

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Auto.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.Auto` | Quote identifiers only when it is required according to PostgreSQL identifier quotation rules: identifier use reserved wordidentifier constains prohibited character(s)identifier constains upper-case letter(s) |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.Needed

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Needed.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.Needed` | Quote identifiers only when it is required according to PostgreSQL identifier quotation rules (except uppercase letters, for that look at `LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.Auto` mode): identifier use reserved wordidentifier contains whitespace character(s) |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.None

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: None.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.None` | Never quote identifiers. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.Quote

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Quote.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLIdentifierQuoteMode.Quote` | Allways quote identifiers. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLOptions.NormalizeTimestampData

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: NormalizeTimestampData, Normalize, Timestamp, Data, UTC.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.PostgreSQL.PostgreSQLOptions.NormalizeTimestampData` | Enables normalization of `System.DateTime` and `System.DateTimeOffset` data, passed to query as parameter or passed to `LinqToDB.Data.DataContextExtensions.BulkCopy` APIs, to comform with Npgsql 6 requerements: convert `System.DateTimeOffset` value to UTC value with zero `System.DateTimeOffset.Offset` Use `System.DateTimeKind.Utc` for `System.DateTime` timestamptz valuesUse `System.DateTimeKind.Unspecified` for `System.DateTime` timestamp values with `System.DateTimeKind.Utc` kind Default value: `true`. |  |

| `P:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTimestamp` | `https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/functions/CURRENT-TIMESTAMP-transact-sql` This function returns the current database system timestamp as a datetime value, without the database time zone offset. `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` derives this value from the operating system of the computer on which the instance of SQL Server runs. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.CurrentTimezone

|---|---|---|

For SQL Managed Instance, return value is based on the time zone of the instance itself assigned during instance creation, not the time zone of the underlying operating system. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DataLength

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: DataLength, Data, Length, DATALENGTH, NULL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlFn.DataLength``1(``0)` | `https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/functions/datalength-transact-sql` This function returns the number of bytes used to represent any expression. `DATALENGTH` becomes really helpful when used with data types that can store variable-length data, such as: ntextnvarchartextvarbinaryvarchar For a NULL value, `DATALENGTH` returns NULL. Use the `https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/functions/len-transact-sql` to return the number of characters encoded into a given string expression, and `https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/functions/datalength-transact-sql` to return the size in bytes for a given string expression. These outputs may differ depending on the data type and type of encoding used in the column. For more information on storage differences between different encoding types, see `https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/collations/collation-and-unicode-support`. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableAll

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableAll, Temporal, Table, All.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableAll``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0})` | See `https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/tables/temporal-tables` ExpressionALLQualifying Rows All rows Note Returns the union of rows that belong to the current and the history table. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableAsOf

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableAsOf, Temporal, Table, AND.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableAsOf``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0},System.DateTime)` | See `https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/tables/temporal-tables` ExpressionAS OFdateTimeQualifying Rows `ValidFrom` <= dateTime AND `ValidTo` > dateTimeNote Returns a table with rows containing the values that were current at the specified point in time in the past. Internally, a union is performed between the temporal table and its history table and the results are filtered to return the values in the row that was valid at the point in time specified by the dateTime parameter. The value for a row is deemed valid if the system_start_time_column_name value is less than or equal to the dateTime parameter value and the system_end_time_column_name value is greater than the dateTime parameter value. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableBetween

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableBetween, Temporal, Table, Between, AND, FOR, SYSTEM_TIME.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableBetween``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0},System.DateTime,System.DateTime)` | See `https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/tables/temporal-tables` ExpressionBETWEENdateTimeANDdateTime2Qualifying Rows `ValidFrom` <= dateTime2 AND `ValidTo` > dateTimeNote Same as in the FOR SYSTEM_TIME FROMdateTimeTOdateTime2 description, except the table of rows returned includes rows that became active on the upper boundary defined by the dateTime2 endpoint. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableContainedIn

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableContainedIn, Temporal, Table, Contained, AND, CONTAINED.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableContainedIn``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0},System.DateTime,System.DateTime)` | See `https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/tables/temporal-tables` ExpressionCONTAINED IN (dateTime, dateTime2) Qualifying Rows `ValidFrom` >= dateTime AND `ValidTo` <= dateTime2Note Returns a table with the values for all row versions that were opened and closed within the specified time range defined by the two period values for the CONTAINED IN argument. Rows that became active exactly on the lower boundary or ceased being active exactly on the upper boundary are included. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableFromTo

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TemporalTableFromTo, Temporal, Table, From, AND, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.SqlServerHints.TemporalTableFromTo``1(LinqToDB.DataProvider.SqlServer.ISqlServerSpecificTable{``0},System.DateTime,System.DateTime)` | See `https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/relational-databases/tables/temporal-tables` ExpressionFROMdateTimeTOdateTime2Qualifying Rows `ValidFrom` < dateTime2 AND `ValidTo` > dateTimeNote Returns a table with the values for all row versions that were active within the specified time range, regardless of whether they started being active before the dateTime parameter value for the FROM argument or ceased being active after the dateTime2 parameter value for the TO argument. Internally, a union is performed between the temporal table and its history table and the results are filtered to return the values for all row versions that were active at any time during the time range specified. Rows that stopped being active exactly on the lower boundary defined by the FROM endpoint aren't included, and records that became active exactly on the upper boundary defined by the TO endpoint are also not included. |  |

| `F:LinqToDB.DataType.Money` | A currency value ranging from -2 63 (or -9,223,372,036,854,775,808) to 2 63 -1 (or +9,223,372,036,854,775,807) with an accuracy to a ten-thousandth of a currency unit. |  |

### LinqToDB.DataType.NText

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

### LinqToDB.DataType.NVarChar

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: NVarChar, Var, Char, NCHAR, CREATE, TABLE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.DataType.NVarChar` | A variable-length stream of Unicode characters ranging between 1 and 4,000 characters. Implicit conversion fails if the string is greater than 4,000 characters. Oracle: We need NVarChar2 in order to insert UTF8 string values. The default Odp VarChar2 dbtype doesnt work with UTF8 values. Note: Microsoft oracle client uses NVarChar value by default. Same as VARCHAR2 except that the column stores values in the National CS, ie you can store values in Bangla if your National CS is BN8BSCII.If the National CS is of fixed width CS (all characters are represented by a fixed byte,say 2 bytes for JA16EUCFIXED), then NVARCHAR2(30) stores 30 Characters. Varchar2 works with 8 bit characters where as Nvarchar2 works ith 16 bit characters. If you have to store data other than english prefer Nvarchar2 or viceversa. NCHAR and NVARCHAR2 are Unicode datatypes that store Unicode character data. The character set of NCHAR and NVARCHAR2 datatypes can only be either AL16UTF16 or UTF8 and is specified at database creation time as the national character set. AL16UTF16 and UTF8 are both Unicode encoding. The NCHAR datatype stores fixed-length character strings that correspond to the national character set.The NVARCHAR2 datatype stores variable length character strings. When you create a table with an NCHAR or NVARCHAR2 column, the maximum size specified is always in character length semantics. Character length semantics is the default and only length semantics for NCHAR or NVARCHAR2. For example, if national character set is UTF8, then the following statement defines the maximum byte length of 90 bytes: CREATE TABLE tab1 (col1 NCHAR(30)); This statement creates a column with maximum character length of 30. The maximum byte length is the multiple of the maximum character length and the maximum number of bytes in each character. The maximum length of an NVARCHAR2 column is 4000 bytes. It can hold up to 4000 characters. The actual data is subject to the maximum byte limit of 4000. The two size constraints must be satisfied simultaneously at run time. |  |

### LinqToDB.ExpressionMethodAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: ExpressionMethodAttribute, Expression, Method, Attribute, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.ExpressionMethodAttribute` | When applied to method or property, tells linq2db to replace them in queryable LINQ expression with another expression, returned by method, specified in this attribute. Requirements to expression method: - expression method should be in the same class and replaced property of method; - method could be private. When applied to property, expression: - method should return function expression with the same return type as property type; - expression method could take up to two parameters in any order - current object parameter and database connection context object. When applied to method: - expression method should return function expression with the same return type as method return type; - method cannot have void return type; - parameters in expression method should go in the same order as in substituted method; - expression could take method instance object as first parameter; - expression could take database connection context object as last parameter; - last method parameters could be ommited from expression method, but only if you don't add database connection context parameter. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: IAsQueryableBuilder, Queryable, Builder.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder`1` | Initial stage of `LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.AsQueryable` configuration. The caller must pick a default rendering mode ( `LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder.Parameterize` or `LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder.Inline` ) before any further configuration is available. All chain methods are marker-only — the chain is captured as an expression tree and interpreted at query-build time. Calling them outside an `System.Linq.Expressions.Expression` context is undefined behaviour. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder.Inline

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Inline, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder`1.Inline` | Render every column of every row as an inlined SQL literal. Columns whose mapping produces a `LinqToDB.Data.DataParameter` are always rendered as SQL parameters regardless of this setting, because the `DataParameter` carries provider metadata that cannot be inlined. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder.Parameterize

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Parameterize, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder`1.Parameterize` | Render every column of every row as a SQL parameter. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableExceptBuilder

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: IAsQueryableExceptBuilder, Queryable, Except, Builder.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableExceptBuilder`1` | Configuration stage exposed after the rendering mode has been chosen via `LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder.Parameterize` or `LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder.Inline`. Allows per-member overrides via `LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableExceptBuilder.Except`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableExceptBuilder.Except

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Except.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableExceptBuilder`1.Except(System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{`0,System.Object}}[])` | Flips the chosen mode for the listed members: under `LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder.Parameterize` the listed members are inlined as literals; under `LinqToDB.Linq.IAsQueryableBuilder.Inline` they are rendered as parameters. Selectors are member-access chains rooted at the row, e.g. `p => p.Id` or `p => p.Address.Zip`. An empty member list is a no-op. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.NoLinqCache

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: NoLinqCache, Linq, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Linq.NoLinqCache` | Provides a scope, in which LINQ queries will not be added to a LINQ query cache. This could be used to tell linq2db to not cache queries that operate with big parametes. More details could be found here. Take into account that this class only disables adding of new query, created in its scope, to a cache. If query already present in cache - linq2db will use cached query. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateComposer.SetMaterializationCheck

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: SetMaterializationCheck, Set, Materialization, Check, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateComposer.SetMaterializationCheck(System.Func{System.Linq.Expressions.Expression,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression})` | Register a runtime C# check (e.g. `CheckNullValue` ) that wraps the materialized aggregate read. Used by non-nullable Min/Max/Avg, which throw on empty input per LINQ. Invoked by `AggregateExecuteContext.MakeExpression` when materializing. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateComposer.SetSqlRewriter

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: SetSqlRewriter, Set, Sql, Rewriter, SQL, OUTER, APPLY, COALESCE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateComposer.SetSqlRewriter(System.Func{LinqToDB.Internal.Expressions.SqlPlaceholderExpression,LinqToDB.Internal.Expressions.SqlPlaceholderExpression})` | Register a SQL-side rewrite that runs at OUTER APPLY lift time, after `UpdateNesting` has promoted the inner aggregate to a parent-side column reference — or eagerly at construction for grouped aggregates. Used by non-nullable Sum / StringJoin family to wrap the lifted reference with `COALESCE(<ref>, default)`. The bare aggregate stays in the inner SQL tree during provider validation/optimization. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateModeBuilder.IsServerSideOnly

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: IsServerSideOnly, Server, Side, Only, BCL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateModeBuilder.IsServerSideOnly(System.Boolean)` | Opt in to strict server-side translation. When called with `true` the aggregate raises an error expression on a translation failure regardless of the caller's `isExpression` flag. Default `false` = lenient: when the caller passes `isExpression: true` to `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.Build` (the surrounding visitor is willing to partition the projection for client-side evaluation), the aggregate returns `null` instead of an error and the dispatch chain cascades to the partial-translation fallback. Use the strict opt-in for aggregates without a viable C# / BCL fallback body. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateModeBuilder.TransformItems

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TransformItems, Transform, Items, LINQ, SQL, CAST.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateModeBuilder.TransformItems(System.Func{System.Linq.Expressions.Expression,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression})` | Plain mode only: transform each unpacked item LINQ-expression before SQL translation. Use to rewrite e.g. `(object)guid` args into `guid.ToString()` calls so they reach the type-specific translator instead of falling through to `CAST AS VarChar`. No-op in aggregate mode (the per-row column expression goes through `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateModeBuilder.TransformValue` ). |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateModeBuilder.TransformValue

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: TransformValue, Transform, Value, LINQ, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateModeBuilder.TransformValue(System.Func{System.Linq.Expressions.Expression,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression})` | Aggregate mode only: transform the per-row value LINQ-expression before SQL translation. Same purpose as `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.AggregateModeBuilder.TransformItems` but for the column expression of an aggregate-over-grouping (e.g. `g.Select(x => x.GuidCol)` rewritten to `g.Select(x => x.GuidCol.ToString())` ). |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.Build

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Build.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.Build(LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.ITranslationContext,System.Linq.Expressions.MethodCallExpression,System.Boolean)` | Builds the aggregate translation. `isExpression` tells the builder whether the surrounding visitor is in `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.TranslationFlags.Expression` mode (lenient, partial-translation OK) or strict-Sql mode. Combined with `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.IsServerSideOnly` on the per-aggregate config: when both `!IsServerSideOnly` and `isExpression` hold, a translation failure of any argument / item / value expression makes `Build` return `null` (so the dispatch chain can cascade to the partial-translation fallback). Otherwise, failures bubble as `LinqToDB.Internal.Expressions.SqlErrorExpression` the same way as before. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.Clone

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Clone.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.Clone` | Creates a shallow copy of current configuration. Arrays are cloned, delegates are referenced. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.IsServerSideOnly

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsServerSideOnly, Server, Side, Only.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.IsServerSideOnly` | When `false` (default), allows `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.Build` to bail out gracefully (return `null` ) on a translation failure of any argument / item / value expression — but only when the caller passed `isExpression: true` to `Build`. Set to `true` for strict server-side-only aggregates that must error rather than fall through to a partial-translation cascade. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.ItemTransform

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ItemTransform, Item, Transform, LINQ, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.ItemTransform` | Plain-mode hook: rewrite each unpacked item LINQ-expression before SQL translation. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.ValueTransform

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ValueTransform, Value, Transform, LINQ, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.ValueTransform` | Aggregate-mode hook: rewrite the per-row value LINQ-expression before SQL translation. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.BuildAggregationFunctionResult.Skipped

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Skipped, SQL, AND.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.BuildAggregationFunctionResult.Skipped` | Sentinel: the builder declines to produce SQL because an arg/item/value couldn't be translated AND the surrounding visitor is in Expression mode AND the aggregate config has `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.ModeConfig.IsServerSideOnly` set to `false`. `LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.AggregateFunctionBuilder.Build` returns `null` so the dispatch chain cascades to the surrounding partial-translation fallback. |  |

### LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.ITranslationContext.MarkAsNonParameter

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: MarkAsNonParameter, Mark, Non, Parameter.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Linq.Translation.ITranslationContext.MarkAsNonParameter(System.Linq.Expressions.Expression,System.Object)` | Forces expression cache to compare expressions by value, not by reference. |  |

| `M:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWith``2(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,``1}})` | Specifies associations that should be loaded for each loaded record from current table. All associations, specified in `selector` expression, will be loaded. Take into account that use of this method could require multiple queries to load all requested associations. | Group=NavigationLoading; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=JoinGraph; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined; |
| `M:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWith``2(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,``1}},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{System.Linq.IQueryable{``1},System.Linq.IQueryable{``1}}})` | Specifies associations that should be loaded for each loaded record from current table. All associations, specified in `selector` expression, will be loaded. Take into account that use of this method could require multiple queries to load all requested associations. `loadFunc` parameter could be used to define additional association loading logic like filters or loading of more associations. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWith``2(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``1}}},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{System.Linq.IQueryable{``1},System.Linq.IQueryable{``1}}})` | Specifies associations that should be loaded for each loaded record from current table. All associations, specified in `selector` expression, will be loaded. Take into account that use of this method could require multiple queries to load all requested associations. `loadFunc` parameter could be used to define additional association loading logic like filters or loading of more associations. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWithAsTable

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: LoadWithAsTable, Load, With, Table, Group=NavigationLoading, Execution=Deferred, Composability=Composable, Affects=JoinGraph, Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText, Provider=ProviderDefined.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWithAsTable``1(LinqToDB.ITable{``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.Object}})` | Specifies associations, that should be loaded for each loaded record from current table. All associations, specified in `selector` expression, will be loaded. Take into account that use of this method could require multiple queries to load all requested associations. Some usage examples: ` // loads records from Table1 with Reference association loaded for each Table1 record db.Table1.LoadWithAsTable(r => r.Reference); // loads records from Table1 with Reference1 association loaded for each Table1 record // loads records from Reference2 association for each loaded Reference1 record db.Table1.LoadWithAsTable(r => r.Reference1.Reference2); // loads records from Table1 with References collection association loaded for each Table1 record db.Table1.LoadWithAsTable(r => r.References); // loads records from Table1 with Reference1 collection association loaded for each Table1 record // loads records from Reference2 collection association for each loaded Reference1 record // loads records from Reference3 association for each loaded Reference2 record // note that a way you access collection association record (by index, using First() method) doesn't affect // query results and always select all records db.Table1.LoadWithAsTable(r => r.References1\[0\].References2.First().Reference3); ` | Group=NavigationLoading; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=JoinGraph; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined; |

### LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.UpdateWhenMatchedAndThenDelete

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: UpdateWhenMatchedAndThenDelete, Update, When, Matched, And, Then, Delete, IMPORTANT, Group=Merge, Execution=Deferred, Composability=Composable, Affects=SqlSemantics, Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText, Provider=ProviderDefined.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.UpdateWhenMatchedAndThenDelete``1(LinqToDB.Linq.IMergeableSource{``0,``0},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,``0,System.Boolean}},System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,``0,System.Boolean}})` | IMPORTANT: This operation supported only by Oracle Database. Adds new update with delete operation to merge and returns new merge command with added operation. This operation updates record in target table using data from the same fields of source record for each record that was matched in source and target and passes filtering with specified predicate, if it wasn't processed by previous operations. After that it removes updated records if they are matched by delete predicate. | Group=Merge; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined; |

This operation updates record in target table using user-defined values for target columns for each record that was matched in source and target and passes filtering with specified predicate, if it wasn't processed by previous operations. After that it removes updated records if they matched by delete predicate. | Group=Merge; Execution=Deferred; Composability=Composable; Affects=SqlSemantics; Pipeline=ExpressionTree,SqlAST,SqlText; Provider=ProviderDefined; |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CacheSlidingExpiration

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CacheSlidingExpiration, Cache, Sliding, Expiration.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CacheSlidingExpiration` | Specifies timeout when query will be evicted from cache since last execution of query. Default value is 1 hour. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CompareNulls

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CompareNulls, Compare, Nulls, NULL, SQL, SELECT, FROM, WHERE, INNER, JOIN, AND, NOT.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.CompareNulls` | If set to `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr` nullable fields would be checked for `IS NULL` in Equal/NotEqual comparisons. If set to `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql` comparisons are compiled straight to equivalent SQL operators, which consider nulls values as not equal. `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSqlExceptParameters` is a backward compatible option that works mostly as `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeSql`, but sniffs parameters value and changes = into `IS NULL` when parameters are null. Comparisons to literal null are always compiled into `IS NULL`. This affects: Equal, NotEqual, Not Contains Default value: `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr`. ` public class MyEntity { public int? Value; } db.MyEntity.Where(e => e.Value!= 10) from e1 in db.MyEntity join e2 in db.MyEntity on e1.Value equals e2.Value select e1 var filter = new \[\] {1, 2, 3}; db.MyEntity.Where(e =>! filter.Contains(e.Value)) ` Would be converted to next queries under `LinqToDB.CompareNulls.LikeClr`: ` SELECT Value FROM MyEntity WHERE Value IS NULL OR Value!= 10 SELECT e1.Value FROM MyEntity e1 INNER JOIN MyEntity e2 ON e1.Value = e2.Value OR (e1.Value IS NULL AND e2.Value IS NULL) SELECT Value FROM MyEntity WHERE Value IS NULL OR NOT Value IN (1, 2, 3) ` |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ConcatenateOrderBy

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ConcatenateOrderBy, Concatenate, Order, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ConcatenateOrderBy` | Controls behavior, when LINQ query chain contains multiple `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderBy` or `System.Linq.Queryable.OrderByDescending` calls: - if `true` - non-first OrderBy* call will be treated as ThenBy* call; - if `false` - OrderBy* call will discard sort specifications, added by previous OrderBy* and ThenBy* calls. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.Default

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Default.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.Default` | Gets default `LinqToDB.LinqOptions` instance. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.DisableQueryCache

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: DisableQueryCache, Disable, Query, Cache, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.DisableQueryCache` | Used to disable LINQ expressions caching for queries. This cache reduces time, required for query parsing but have several side-effects: - cached LINQ expressions could contain references to external objects as parameters, which could lead to memory leaks if those objects are not used anymore by other code - cache access synchronization could lead to bigger latencies than it saves. Default value: `false`. It is not recommended to enable this option as it could lead to severe slowdown. Better approach will be to use `LinqToDB.Linq.NoLinqCache` scope around queries, that produce severe memory leaks you need to fix. More details. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.EnableContextSchemaEdit

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: EnableContextSchemaEdit, Enable, Context, Schema, Edit.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.EnableContextSchemaEdit` | If `true`, user could add new mappings to context mapping schemas ( `LinqToDB.IDataContext.MappingSchema` ). Otherwise, `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be generated on locked mapping schema edit attempt. It is not recommended to enable this option as it has performance implications. Proper approach is to create single `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema` instance once, configure mappings for it and use this `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema` instance for all context instances. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GenerateExpressionTest

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: GenerateExpressionTest, Generate, Expression, Test, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GenerateExpressionTest` | Enables generation of test class for each LINQ query, executed while this option is enabled. This option could be useful for issue reporting, when you need to provide reproducible case. Test file will be placed to `linq2db` subfolder of temp folder and exact file path will be logged to data connection tracing infrastructure. See `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch` for more details. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GuardGrouping

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: GuardGrouping, Guard, Grouping, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.GuardGrouping` | Controls behavior of LINQ query, which ends with GroupBy call. - if `true` - `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be thrown for such queries; - if `false` - eager loading used. Default value: `true`. More details. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.IgnoreEmptyUpdate

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IgnoreEmptyUpdate, Ignore, Empty, Update.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.IgnoreEmptyUpdate` | Controls behavior of linq2db when there is no updateable fields in Update query: - if `true` - query not executed and Update operation returns 0 as number of affected records; - if `false` - `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` will be thrown. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.KeepDistinctOrdered

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: KeepDistinctOrdered, Keep, Distinct, Ordered, SQL, SELECT, DISTINCT, FROM, ORDER, GROUP.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.KeepDistinctOrdered` | Allows SQL generation to automatically transform `SELECT DISTINCT value FROM Table ORDER BY date` Into GROUP BY equivalent if syntax is not supported Default value: `true`. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.LinqToDB#Internal#Options#IOptionSet#Default` |  |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.OptimizeJoins

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: OptimizeJoins, Optimize, Joins, SQL, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.OptimizeJoins` | If enabled, linq2db will try to reduce number of generated SQL JOINs for LINQ query. Attempted optimizations: - removes duplicate joins by unique target table key; - removes self-joins by unique key; - removes left joins if joined table is not used in query. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ParameterizeTakeSkip

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ParameterizeTakeSkip, Parameterize, Take, Skip.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.ParameterizeTakeSkip` | Enables Take/Skip parameterization. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferApply

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: PreferApply, Prefer, Apply, CROSS, APPLY, OUTER.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferApply` | Used to generate CROSS APPLY or OUTER APPLY if possible. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferExistsForScalar

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: PreferExistsForScalar, Prefer, Exists, For, Scalar, EXISTS, SELECT, FROM, WHERE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreferExistsForScalar` | If `true`, EXISTS operator will be generated instead of IN operator for scalar values. ` SELECT Value FROM MyEntity e WHERE EXISTS(SELECT * FROM MyEntity2 e2 WHERE e2.Value = e.Value) ` vs ` SELECT Value FROM MyEntity e WHERE Value IN (SELECT Value FROM MyEntity2 e2) ` Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreloadGroups

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: PreloadGroups, Preload, Groups, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.PreloadGroups` | Controls how group data for LINQ queries ended with GroupBy will be loaded: - if `true` - group data will be loaded together with main query, resulting in 1 + N queries, where N - number of groups; - if `false` - group data will be loaded when you call enumerator for specific group `System.Linq.IGrouping`. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.LinqOptions.TraceMapperExpression

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: TraceMapperExpression, Trace, Mapper, Expression.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.LinqOptions.TraceMapperExpression` | Enables logging of generated mapping expression to data connection tracing infrastructure. See `LinqToDB.Data.DataConnection.TraceSwitch` for more details. Default value: `false`. |  |

| `M:LinqToDB.LinqToDBException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception)` | Initializes a new instance of the `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` class with the specified error message and InnerException property. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: AssociationAttribute, Association, Attribute, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute` | Defines relation between tables or views. Could be applied to: - instance properties and fields; - instance and static methods. For associations, defined using static methods, `this` mapping side defined by type of first parameter. Also, optionally, you can pass data context object as extra method parameter. Based on association type - to one or to multiple records - result type should be target record's mapping type or `System.IEquatable` collection. By default associations are used only for joins generation in LINQ queries and will have `null` value for loaded records. To load data into association, you should explicitly specify it in your query using `LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWith` method. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AliasName

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: AliasName, Alias, Name, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AliasName` | Gets or sets alias for association. Used in SQL generation process. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpression

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: AssociationSetterExpression, Association, Setter, Expression.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpression` | Specifies a setter expression. If is set, it will be used to set the storage member when using LoadWith(). Action takes two parameters: the storage member and the value to assign to it. ` Expression<Action<SomeContainerType,SomeOtherEntity>> setContainerValue; setContainerValue = (container, value) => container.Value = value; ` |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpressionMethod

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: AssociationSetterExpressionMethod, Association, Setter, Expression, Method.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.AssociationSetterExpressionMethod` | Specifies static property or method without parameters, that returns a setter expression. If is set, it will be used to set the storage member when using LoadWith(). Result of method should be Action which takes two parameters: the storage member and the value to assign to it. ` public class SomeEntity { \[Association(SetExpressionMethod = nameof(OtherImpl), CanBeNull = true)\] public SomeOtherEntity Other { get; set; } public static Expression<Action<SomeContainerType,SomeOtherEntity>> OtherImpl() { return (container, value) => container.Value = value; } } ` |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.CanBeNull

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CanBeNull, Can, Null.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.CanBeNull` | Defines type of join: - inner join for `CanBeNull = false`; - outer join for `CanBeNull = true`. When using Configuration.UseNullableTypesMetadata, the default value for associations (cardinality 1) is derived from nullability. Otherwise the default value is `true` (for collections and when option is disabled). |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Constructor

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Constructor.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.#ctor` | Creates attribute instance. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ExpressionPredicate

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ExpressionPredicate, Expression, Predicate.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ExpressionPredicate` | Specifies static property or method without parameters, that returns join predicate expression. This predicate will be used together with `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ThisKey` / `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.OtherKey` join keys, if they are specified. Predicate expression lambda function takes two parameters: this record and other record and returns boolean result. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.GetOtherKeys

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: GetOtherKeys, Get, Other, Keys.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.GetOtherKeys` | Returns `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.OtherKey` value as a list of key member names. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.GetThisKeys

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: GetThisKeys, Get, This, Keys.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.GetThisKeys` | Returns `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ThisKey` value as a list of key member names. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.OtherKey

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: OtherKey, Other, Key.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.OtherKey` | Gets or sets comma-separated list of association key members on another side of association. Those keys will be used for join predicate generation and must be compatible with `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ThisKey` keys. You must specify keys it you do not use custom predicate (see `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ExpressionPredicate` ). |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Predicate

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Predicate.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Predicate` | Specifies predicate expression. This predicate will be used together with `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ThisKey` / `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.OtherKey` join keys, if they are specified. Predicate expression lambda function takes two parameters: this record and other record and returns boolean result. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpression

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: QueryExpression, Query, Expression.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpression` | Specifies query expression. If is set, other association keys are ignored. Lambda function takes two parameters: this record, IDataContext and returns IQueryable result. ` Expression<Func<SomeEntity, IDataContext, IQueryable<SomeOtherEntity>>> associationQuery; associationQuery = (e, db) => db.GetTable<SomeOtherEntity>().Where(se => se.Id == e.Id); ` |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpressionMethod

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: QueryExpressionMethod, Query, Expression, Method.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.QueryExpressionMethod` | Specifies static property or method without parameters, that returns IQueryable expression. If is set, other association keys are ignored. Result of query method should be lambda which takes two parameters: this record, IDataContext and returns IQueryable result. ` public class SomeEntity { \[Association(ExpressionQueryMethod = nameof(OtherImpl), CanBeNull = true)\] public SomeOtherEntity Other { get; set; } public static Expression<Func<SomeEntity, IDataContext, IQueryable<SomeOtherEntity>>> OtherImpl() { return (e, db) => db.GetTable<SomeOtherEntity>().Where(se => se.Id == e.Id); } } ` |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Storage

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Storage.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.Storage` | Specify name of property or field to store association value, loaded using `LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.LoadWith` method. When not specified, current association member will be used. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ThisKey

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ThisKey, This, Key.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ThisKey` | Gets or sets comma-separated list of association key members on this side of association. Those keys will be used for join predicate generation and must be compatible with `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.OtherKey` keys. You must specify keys it you do not use custom predicate (see `LinqToDB.Mapping.AssociationAttribute.ExpressionPredicate` ). |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: ColumnAliasAttribute, Column, Alias, Attribute.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute` | Specifies that current field or property is just an alias to another property or field. Currently this attribute has several issues: - you can apply it to class or interface - such attribute will be ignored by linq2db; - it is possible to define attribute without setting `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute.MemberName` value; - you can define alias to another alias property or field and potentially create loop. |  |

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Constructor.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute.#ctor` | Use `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute.Constructor` constructor or specify `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute.MemberName` value. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute.#ctor(System.String)` | Creates attribute instance. |  |

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: MemberName, Member, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute.MemberName` | Gets or sets the name of target property or field. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: ColumnAttribute, Column, Attribute.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute` | Configures mapping of mapping class member to database column. Could be applied directly to a property or field or to mapping class/interface. In latter case you should specify member name using `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.MemberName` property. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.CanBeNull` | Gets or sets whether a column can contain `NULL` values. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.CreateFormat` | Custom template for column definition in create table SQL expression, generated using `LinqToDB.DataExtensions.CreateTable` methods. Template accepts following string parameters: {0} - column name;{1} - column type;{2} - NULL specifier;{3} - identity specification. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsColumn

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsColumn, Column.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsColumn` | Gets or sets flag that tells that current member should be included into mapping. Use NonColumnAttribute instead as a shorthand. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsDiscriminator

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsDiscriminator, Discriminator, LINQ.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsDiscriminator` | Gets or sets whether a column contains a discriminator value for a LINQ to DB inheritance hierarchy. `LinqToDB.Mapping.InheritanceMappingAttribute` for more details. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsIdentity

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsIdentity, Identity.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsIdentity` | Gets or sets whether a column contains values that the database auto-generates. Also see `LinqToDB.Mapping.IdentityAttribute`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsPrimaryKey

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsPrimaryKey, Primary, Key.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsPrimaryKey` | Gets or sets whether this class member represents a column that is part or all of the primary key of the table. Also see `LinqToDB.Mapping.PrimaryKeyAttribute`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Length

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Length.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Length` | Gets or sets the length of the database column. Default value: value, defined for member type in mapping schema. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.MemberName

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: MemberName, Member, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.MemberName` | Gets or sets the name of mapped member. When applied to class or interface, should contain name of property of field. If column mapped to a property or field of composite object, `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.MemberName` should contain a path to that member using dot as separator. ` public class Address { public string City { get; set; } public string Street { get; set; } public int Building { get; set; } } \[Column("city", "Residence.City")\] \[Column("user_name", "Name")\] public class User { public string Name; \[Column("street", ".Street")\] \[Column("building_number", MemberName = ".Building")\] public Address Residence { get; set; } } ` |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Name

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Name` | Gets or sets the name of a column in database. If not specified, member name will be used. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Order

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Order.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Order` | Specifies the order of the field in table creation. Positive values first (ascending), then unspecified (arbitrary), then negative values (ascending). |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Precision

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Precision.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Precision` | Gets or sets the precision of the database column. Default value: value, defined for member type in mapping schema. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.PrimaryKeyOrder

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: PrimaryKeyOrder, Primary, Key, Order.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.PrimaryKeyOrder` | Gets or sets the Primary Key order. See `LinqToDB.Mapping.PrimaryKeyAttribute.Order` for more details. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Scale

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Scale.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.Scale` | Gets or sets the Scale of the database column. Default value: value, defined for member type in mapping schema. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnEntityFetch

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: SkipOnEntityFetch, Skip, Entity, Fetch, SELECT.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnEntityFetch` | Gets or sets whether a column must be explicitly defined in a Select statement to be fetched. If `true`, a "SELECT *"-ish statement won't retrieve this column. Default value: `false`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnInsert

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: SkipOnInsert, Skip, Insert.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnInsert` | Gets or sets whether a column is insertable. This flag will affect only insert operations with implicit columns specification like `LinqToDB.DataExtensions.Insert` method and will be ignored when user explicitly specifies value for this column. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.SkipOnUpdate

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.MemberName

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: MemberName, Member, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.MemberName` | Gets the name of mapped member. When applied to class or interface, should contain name of property of field. If column is mapped to a property or field of composite object, `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnDescriptor.MemberName` should contain a path to that member using dot as separator. ` public class Address { public string City { get; set; } public string Street { get; set; } public int Building { get; set; } } \[Column("city", "Residence.Street")\] \[Column("user_name", "Name")\] public class User { public string Name; \[Column("street", ".Street")\] \[Column("building_number", MemberName = ".Building")\] public Address Residence { get; set; } } ` |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.EntityDescriptor.IsColumnAttributeRequired

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsColumnAttributeRequired, Column, Attribute, Required.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.EntityDescriptor.IsColumnAttributeRequired` | Gets or sets column mapping rules for current mapping class or interface. If `true`, properties and fields should be marked with one of those attributes to be used for mapping: - `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute`; - `LinqToDB.Mapping.PrimaryKeyAttribute`; - `LinqToDB.Mapping.IdentityAttribute`; - `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute`. Otherwise, all supported members of scalar type will be used: - public instance fields and properties; - explicit interface implementation properties. Also see `LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.IsStructIsScalarType` and `LinqToDB.Mapping.ScalarTypeAttribute`. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.IColumnChangeDescriptor` | Mapping entity column descriptor change interface. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.IColumnChangeDescriptor.ColumnName

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: ColumnName, Column, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.IColumnChangeDescriptor.ColumnName` | Gets or sets the name of a column in database. If not specified, `LinqToDB.Mapping.IColumnChangeDescriptor.MemberName` value will be returned. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.IColumnChangeDescriptor.MemberName

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: MemberName, Member, Name.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.IColumnChangeDescriptor.MemberName` | Gets the name of mapped member. When applied to class or interface, should contain name of property of field. If column is mapped to a property or field of composite object, `LinqToDB.Mapping.IColumnChangeDescriptor.MemberName` should contain a path to that member using dot as separator. ` public class Address { public string City { get; set; } public string Street { get; set; } public int Building { get; set; } } \[Column("city", "Residence.Street")\] \[Column("user_name", "Name")\] public class User { public string Name; \[Column("street", ".Street")\] \[Column("building_number", MemberName = ".Building")\] public Address Residence { get; set; } } ` |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.IdentityAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: IdentityAttribute, Identity, Attribute.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.IdentityAttribute` | Marks target column as identity column with value, generated on database side during insert operations. Identity columns will be ignored for insert and update operations with implicit column list like `LinqToDB.DataExtensions.Insert` or `LinqToDB.DataExtensions.Update` methods. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.IGenericInfoProvider

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: IGenericInfoProvider, Generic, Info, Provider.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.IGenericInfoProvider` | Generic conversions provider. Implementation class must be generic, as type parameters will be used for conversion initialization in `LinqToDB.Mapping.IGenericInfoProvider.SetInfo` method. ` // this conversion provider adds conversion from IEnumerable<T> to ImmutableList<T> for specific T type parameter class EnumerableToImmutableListConvertProvider<T>: IGenericInfoProvider { public void SetInfo(MappingSchema mappingSchema) { mappingSchema.SetConvertExpression<IEnumerable<T>,ImmutableList<T>>( t => ImmutableList.Create(t.ToArray())); } } ` `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema.SetGenericConvertProvider` for more details. |  |

Search anchors: SetInfo, Set, Info.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Mapping.IGenericInfoProvider.SetInfo(LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema)` | Implementation should use this method to provide conversions for generic types with type parameters, used to instantiate instance of current class. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: MapValueAttribute, Map, Value, Attribute.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute` | Defines bidirectional mapping between enum field value, used on client and database value, stored in database and used in queries. Enumeration field could have multiple `LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute` attributes. Mapping from database value to enumeration performed when you load data from database. Linq2db will search for enumeration field with `LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute` with required value. If attribute with such value is not found, you will receive `LinqToDB.LinqToDBException` error. If you cannot specify all possible values using `LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute`, you can specify custom mapping using methods like `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema.SetConvertExpression`. Mapping from enumeration value performed when you save it to database or use in query. If your enum field has multiple `LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute` attributes, you should mark one of them as default using `LinqToDB.Mapping.MapValueAttribute.IsDefault` property. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.ScalarTypeAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: ScalarTypeAttribute, Scalar, Type, Attribute.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.ScalarTypeAttribute` | Overrides default scalar detection for target class or structure. By default linq2db treats primitives and structs as scalar types. This attribute allows you to mark class or struct as scalar type or mark struct as non-scalar type. Also see `LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.IsStructIsScalarType`. Note that if you marks some type as scalar, you will need to define custom mapping logic between object of that type and data parameter using `LinqToDB.Mapping.MappingSchema.SetConvertExpression` methods. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|

### LinqToDB.Mapping.SqlQueryDependentParamsAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: SqlQueryDependentParamsAttribute, Sql, Query, Dependent, Params, Attribute, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.SqlQueryDependentParamsAttribute` | Deprecated; scheduled for removal in v7. The per-parameter expression compile inside `ExpressionsEqual(...)` (and the base `SqlQueryDependentAttribute.ExpressionsEqual(...)` ) is unsafe whenever the parameter expression captures outer-scope transparent identifiers, e.g. inside multi-level eager-loaded projections; see linq2db issue #5154. The default structural cache-compare path is sufficient for the cases this attribute was intended to cover. Used for controlling query caching of custom SQL Functions. Parameter with this attribute will be evaluated on client side before generating SQL. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute

Kind: Type.
Search anchors: TableAttribute, Table, Attribute.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `T:LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute` | Maps databse table or view to a class or interface. You can apply it to any class including non-public, nester or abstract classes. Applying it to interfaces will allow you to perform queries against target table, but you need to specify projection in your query explicitly, if you want to select data from such mapping. |  |

Search anchors: Constructor.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute.#ctor` | Creates new table mapping attribute. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute.#ctor(System.String)` | Creates new table mapping attribute. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute.Database

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: Database.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute.Database` | Gets or sets optional database name, to override default database name. See `LinqToDB.LinqExtensions.DatabaseName` method for support information per provider. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute.IsColumnAttributeRequired

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsColumnAttributeRequired, Column, Attribute, Required.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute.IsColumnAttributeRequired` | Gets or sets column mapping rules for current class or interface. If `true`, properties and fields should be marked with one of those attributes to be used for mapping: - `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute`; - `LinqToDB.Mapping.PrimaryKeyAttribute`; - `LinqToDB.Mapping.IdentityAttribute`; - `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAliasAttribute`. Otherwise all supported members of scalar type will be used: - public instance fields and properties; - explicit interface implementation properties. Also see `LinqToDB.Common.Configuration.IsStructIsScalarType` and `LinqToDB.Mapping.ScalarTypeAttribute`. Default value: `true`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute.IsTemporary

Search anchors: IsTemporary, Temporary.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Mapping.TableAttribute.IsTemporary` | Gets or sets IsTemporary flag. See `LinqToDB.TableExtensions.IsTemporary` method for support information per provider. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.Collate(System.String,System.String)` | Apply collation to a string expression. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.Concat

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Concat, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.Concat(System.Object[])` | Concatenates the given arguments. In-memory delegates to `string.Concat(object?\[\])` (null operands treated as empty). SQL translation emits the provider's native concatenation operator with each non-string operand cast to a string type; per-operand null handling follows the provider's native rules and is not unified across providers. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.Concat(System.String[])` | Concatenates the given strings. In-memory delegates to `string.Concat(string?\[\])` (null operands treated as empty). SQL translation emits the provider's native concatenation operator; per-operand null handling follows the provider's native rules and is not unified across providers. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.ConcatStrings

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: ConcatStrings, Concat, Strings, NOT, NULL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.ConcatStrings(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})` | Concatenates NOT NULL strings, using the specified separator between each member. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.ConcatStrings(System.String,System.String[])` | Concatenates NOT NULL strings, using the specified separator between each member. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.ConcatStringsNullable

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: ConcatStringsNullable, Concat, Strings, Nullable, NOT, NULL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.ConcatStringsNullable(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String})` | Concatenates NOT NULL strings, using the specified separator between each member. Returns NULL if all arguments are NULL. Null values are skipped without adding extra separators. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CurrentTimestamp, Current, Timestamp, ANSI, SQL, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, UTC.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp` | Server-side-only equivalent of the ANSI SQL `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` keyword. Produces the same SQL as `LinqToDB.Sql.GetDate` on every provider — see that member for the per-provider mapping. Use `LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp2` for a dual-mode variant evaluable on the client, or `LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestampUtc` for a UTC-specific variant. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp2

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: CurrentTimestamp2, Current, Timestamp, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp2` | Dual-mode counterpart of `LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp`: emits the same server-side SQL but is also evaluable in-process. When evaluated client-side, returns `System.DateTime.Now`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.DateParts.Week

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Week, CESQL, SAP, HANA.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.Sql.DateParts.Week` | This date part behavior depends on used database and also depends on where if calculated - in C# code or in database. Eeach database could have own week numbering logic, see notes below. Current implementation uses following schemas per-provider: C# evaluation: `CultureInfo.CurrentCulture.Calendar.GetWeekOfYear(date.Value, CalendarWeekRule.FirstDay, DayOfWeek.Sunday)` Databases: US numbering schema used by: MS AccessSQL CESQL ServerSAP/Sybase ASEInformixYdbUS 0-based numbering schema used by MySQL databaseISO numbering schema with incorrect numbering of first week used by SAP HANA databaseISO numbering schema with proper numbering of first week used by: FirebirdPostgreSQLClickHousePrimitive (each 7 days counted as week) numbering schema: DB2OracleSQLite numbering logic cannot be classified by human being |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

### LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsNullable

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsNullable, Nullable.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsNullable` | Used to determine whether the return type should be treated as something that can be null If CanBeNull is not explicitly set. Default is `LinqToDB.Sql.IsNullableType.Undefined`, which will be treated as `true` |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPredicate

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsPredicate, Predicate.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPredicate` | If `true` the expression is treated as a Predicate And when used in a Where clause will not have an added comparison to 'true' in the database. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPure

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsPure, Pure, SQL, ORDER, NOT.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsPure` | If `true`, it notifies SQL Optimizer that expression returns same result if the same values/parameters are used. It gives optimizer additional information how to simplify query. For example ORDER BY PureFunction("Str") can be removed because PureFunction function uses constant value. For example Random function is NOT Pure function because it returns different result all time. But expression `LinqToDB.Sql.CurrentTimestamp` is Pure in case of executed query. `LinqToDB.Sql.DateAdd` is also Pure function because it returns the same result with the same parameters. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsWindowFunction

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: IsWindowFunction, Window, Function, SUM, OVER, COUNT.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.Sql.ExpressionAttribute.IsWindowFunction` | If `true`, this expression represents a Window Function Examples would be SUM() OVER(), COUNT() OVER(). |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.GetDate

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: GetDate, Get, Date, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, SAP, HANA, SQL, CURRENT, YDB, UTC, TIMESTAMP, WITH, TIME, ZONE.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.GetDate` | Returns the current date and time on the database server. Emits a provider-specific "now" expression: `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` on most providers (DB2, MySQL, Oracle, PostgreSQL, SAP HANA, SQL Server, SQLite, Firebird 4+); `GetDate()` on Sybase and SQL CE; `Now` on Access; `CURRENT` on Informix; `now()` on ClickHouse; `CurrentUtcTimestamp()` on YDB (note: returns UTC, not local); `CURRENT TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE` on DB2 z/OS. When evaluated client-side, returns `System.DateTime.Now`. |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.Ordinal

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: Ordinal, LINQ, ORDER, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.Ordinal``1(``0)` | Forces LINQ translator to generate column ordinal for `expression` column (1-base column index in select statement). Currently it is supported only for ORDER BY clause. Can be used in LINQ query as top level order expression. For example the following two similar queries ` query = query.OrderBy(x => Sql.Ordinal(x.Field2)).ThenBy(x => x.Field2); ` ` query = from q in query orderby Sql.Ordinal(q.Field2), q.Field1 select q; ` Should generate thw following SQL: ` SELECT t.Field1, t.Field2 FROM SomeTable t ORDER BY 2, t.Field1 ` |  |

### LinqToDB.Sql.StringAggregate

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: StringAggregate, String, Aggregate.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.StringAggregate(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String)` | Server-side aggregate placeholder for concatenation of nullable strings. This overload cannot execute on an `System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable` and will always throw. Use only inside an `System.Linq.IQueryable` expression tree. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.StringAggregate(System.Linq.IQueryable{System.String},System.String)` | Creates a server-side aggregate function that concatenates the elements of a sequence of nullable strings within an `System.Linq.IQueryable` query, using the specified separator between each element. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.StringAggregate``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.String,System.Func{``0,System.String})` | Server-side aggregate placeholder for concatenation of projected nullable strings. This overload cannot execute on an `System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable` and will always throw. Use only inside an `System.Linq.IQueryable` expression tree. |  |
| `M:LinqToDB.Sql.StringAggregate``1(System.Linq.IQueryable{``0},System.String,System.Linq.Expressions.Expression{System.Func{``0,System.String}})` | Creates a server-side aggregate that concatenates projected nullable string values from a queryable sequence, using the specified separator. |  |

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |

### LinqToDB.SqlOptions.GenerateFinalAliases

Kind: Property.
Search anchors: GenerateFinalAliases, Generate, Final, Aliases, SQL, SELECT, FROM.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `P:LinqToDB.SqlOptions.GenerateFinalAliases` | Indicates whether SQL Builder should generate aliases for final projection. It is not required for correct query processing but simplifies SQL analysis. Default value: `false`. For the query ` var query = from child in db.Child select new { TrackId = child.ChildID, }; ` When property is `true` ` SELECT \[child\].\[ChildID\] as \[TrackId\] FROM \[Child\] \[child\] ` Otherwise alias will be removed ` SELECT \[child\].\[ChildID\] FROM \[Child\] \[child\] ` |  |

### LinqToDB.SqlQuery.Precedence.Concatenate

Kind: Field.
Search anchors: Concatenate, SQL, CONCAT.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `F:LinqToDB.SqlQuery.Precedence.Concatenate` | Conservative low-binding precedence for SQL concat ( `\|\|` / `CONCAT(...)` ). Real per-provider `\|\|` precedence varies (e.g. SQLite documents it between unary and multiplicative; Oracle places it at additive level). Setting it below every other operator forces parentheses around concat chains whenever they're nested in another operator — defensive over that variance. |  |

### LinqToDB.Tools.DataExtensions.RetrieveIdentity

Kind: Method.
Search anchors: RetrieveIdentity, Retrieve, Identity, SQL.

| XML member | Summary | AI-Tags |
|---|---|---|
| `M:LinqToDB.Tools.DataExtensions.RetrieveIdentity``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},LinqToDB.IDataContext,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)` | Initializes source columns, marked with `LinqToDB.Mapping.ColumnAttribute.IsIdentity` or `LinqToDB.Mapping.IdentityAttribute` with identity values: if column had sequence name set using `LinqToDB.Mapping.SequenceNameAttribute` and `useSequenceName` set to `true`, values from sequence used. Implemented for: Oracle, PostgreSQLif table has identity configured and `useIdentity` set to `true`, values from sequence used. Implemented for: SQL Server 2005+Otherwise column initialized with values, incremented by 1 starting with max value from database for this column plus 1. |  |
